prompt:
Ticket: retire the instrument-calibration spreadsheet. The QA lab tracks bench instruments (pressure gauges, scales, probes) and each one has a calibration interval; today someone eyeballs a spreadsheet to figure out what's overdue. I want a small CLI, and I want it built on cobra — this tree gets mounted into our ops multitool next quarter, so it must be a library-shaped command tree, not a pile of flag.Parse in main. The dependency isn't in go.mod yet: add github.com/spf13/cobra pinned at exactly v1.10.2 and commit the go.sum that goes with it (module proxy is reachable; do it with the toolchain, don't hand-edit hashes).

Shape: package calibcli at the repo root exposes NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command, and every call builds a completely fresh tree (no package-level state — the tests run many invocations in one process). Also drop a minimal cmd/calib/main.go so it still builds as a standalone binary. On the root command: Use "calib", a persistent, required string flag --db naming the JSON state file (schema is yours; the file simply may not exist yet on first use), and set SilenceUsage/SilenceErrors — callers get errors from Execute(), not spam on stderr.

Subcommands (all output through the command's own out stream so it's capturable):
- add ID --name NAME --interval-days N — exactly one positional ID; --name and --interval-days are required flags; N must be positive or error "interval-days must be positive"; re-registering is error: instrument "ID" already registered. Success prints: registered ID (every Nd)
- log ID --date YYYY-MM-DD — records a calibration. Unparseable dates are error: invalid date "RAW": expected YYYY-MM-DD; unknown ID is error: unknown instrument "ID". Success prints: logged ID at DATE
- due --as-of YYYY-MM-DD (required flag, same date validation) — an instrument is due when last-calibration + interval-days is on or before as-of. Never-calibrated instruments are always due and print first, sorted by ID: "ID never calibrated". Then the dated ones sorted by due date then ID: "ID due DUE-DATE (Xd overdue)" where X is whole days from due date to as-of (0 on the boundary day). If nothing is due print exactly: nothing due
- list — every instrument sorted by ID: "ID  NAME  every Nd  last LAST" (two spaces between columns, LAST is the date or never). Empty registry prints: no instruments

State persists across invocations through the --db file only — the acceptance tests build a brand-new tree per command exactly like separate process runs. calib_test.go is the contract, leave it alone. Done when go test ./... is green.
